column8: Context & Notes
Consolidated: 'Kansas City Call'(10), 'the Call'(100). ~500 MNF columns published; longest-running column in paper's history. Founded October 1919. Mrs. Bluford encouraged Nunnelly to start writing.
Consolidated: 'Lincoln High'(50), 'Lincoln High School'(39). Attended by Clark, Allen, West, Price, Greenfield, Sypert, Ike Gardner. Most important shared institution in MNF.
Consolidated: '18th and Vine'(14), '18th Street'(22), '18th'(38), 'Vine Street'(4), 'Vine'(21). Cultural heart of KC Black community; site of blues/jazz clubs, Call newspaper, Lincoln Building.

Consolidated: 'Langston University'(2), 'Langston'(38). HBCU attended by Sypert and Price. Key pre-KC shared institution. Site of civil rights awakening. Dr. Fisher (first Black to integrate OU law) taught here.
Consolidated: 'Pitt State'(34). Ed West's alma mater on athletic scholarship; Charles Allen also references it.
John Martin's hometown (Sumner High School, class of January 1961). Also referenced by Clark, Sypert.
Group home where Ike Gardner, Walt Livingston, Paul Booker, Harry Sypert, and Charles Allen all worked. Critical secondary bonding institution for MNF.

Consolidated: 'Arrowhead'(22), 'Arrowhead Stadium'(1). Home of KC Chiefs. MNF members held season tickets for decades. Nunnelly especially felt betrayed when Chiefs moved here from the Black community neighborhood.
Consolidated: 'Osawatomie'(20), 'Osawatomie, Kansas'(1). Ed West's childhood home after leaving Clarksville, AR. Father worked for Missouri Pacific Railroad here.
Multiple references across transcripts. Cleaver traveled with Obama to Africa. General civil rights and cultural references by multiple members.

column4: 22nd St & Brooklyn Ave, Kansas City, MO
column8: Original KC Chiefs/Athletics home; in the heart of the Black community. Members attended early games here; longest game memory (Pogolak).
Consolidated: 'Shawnee'(13), 'Shawnee, Oklahoma'(3). Harry Sypert's birthplace and hometown; ~30 miles east of Oklahoma City.
Consolidated: 'California'(14), 'Los Angeles'(2). Ed West and wife relocated here after Yonkers; son Damon's music career was based here.
Major commercial/social street; location of '6th Night of Prospect' current MNF meeting venue.
Gave MNF honorary lifetime membership (1996). Mentioned by Clark, Cleaver, Sypert, Nunnelly, Joseph.
Consolidated: 'Gates'(12). Nunnelly visits every Thursday. Ollie Gates has hosted MNF. Community institution.
Charles Allen's first jazz club. Named after the Sands in Las Vegas. Wilt Chamberlain visited on Fridays from KU.

column4: 2121 George Halas Dr NW, Canton, OH 44708
column8: Consolidated: 'Hall of Fame'(11), 'Canton'(2), 'Canton, Ohio'(1). MNF members traveled here for Buck Buchanan's and Len Dawson's inductions. Buck thanked MNF in his induction speech.
Consolidated: 'DC'(10), 'Congress'(7), 'March on Washington'(1), 'White House'(2). Cleaver's congressional work. Clinton drug policy council. March on Washington reference.
Southern boundary of the historic Black neighborhood corridor.

column4: 31st St & Benton Blvd, Kansas City, MO
column8: Consolidated: '31st'(7), 'Benton'(3). Leon Jordan Civil Rights Memorial; Nunnelly, Ike Gardner, Allen named on it.
Near Municipal Stadium; parking for Chiefs games.
Charles Allen's second jazz club. Named after Charlie Parker. Featured live music.
Ed West — one of first Black supervisors; promoted multiple times. Don Williams also worked here. Nunnelly credits West with helping create early computers.
Consolidated: 'Plaza'(5), 'the plaza'(4). Allen and others mention nightclubs and socializing here.
Consolidated: 'Tulsa'(9). Sypert taught here briefly; civil rights context.
Consolidated: 'Columbia, Missouri'(1), 'Columbia'(7). Jim Nunnelly's birthplace (1942). Segregated hometown where he faced racial pressure to attend the university.
Consolidated: 'University of Missouri'(4), 'Mizzou'(1), 'MU'(many — inflated by substring). Nunnelly one of first ~15 Black students. Greenfield's grandniece earned scholarship here.
Harry Sypert's regional hub; civil rights activity center.

column2: Denton, TX / Fred Moore High School
column8: Consolidated: 'Denton'(6), 'Fred Moore'(2), 'Fred Moore High School'(1). Jerry Price's first coaching job. Faced racial hostility from some parents/staff; left for Kansas City.
Don Williams' birthplace (4th-generation Californian). Cleaver references Oakland Raiders fans. Don Williams flew with Tuskegee Airman John Briggs.
Referenced by Cleaver, Clark, Allen, Sypert, Joseph — civil rights movement and general cultural references.
Consolidated: 'El Capitan'(3), 'El Cap'(4). Across from the Gem Theater on 18th Street.
Jerry Price coached football, girls basketball, and track here. Faced parental racism his first year.
Consolidated: 'Yonkers'(5), 'Yonkers, New York'(2). Ed West and wife Celeste lived here 3 years when Celeste transferred to GM's NY operations.
Referenced by Allen, West, Price. Possible confusion with Pittsburg State (KS) in some instances.
Consolidated: 'Iowa'(5), 'University of Iowa'(2). Jerry Price considered attending here before choosing Langston.
Consolidated: 'Cuba'(7). Cleaver met with Castro twice. Arranged Eldridge Cleaver's return to U.S. after exile in Cuba.
Jerry Price's apartment complex at Linwood Hills, where Ike Gardner recruited him to MNF (~1972).
Charles Allen's street near 18th & Vine; Allen's house and nearby festival grounds.
Jazz venue on 18th & Vine; Charles Allen spent significant time here.
Across from Allen's childhood home at 1613 Campbell; nursery school there.
Housed Black physicians and dentists. Stuart Watson's parents controlled the 3rd floor. Allen had dental office here.
Consolidated: 'Colgate'(4), 'Colgate-Palmolive'(2). Carl Clark's long-term employer; started as labor, rose through apprenticeship.
Consolidated: 'Swope Park'(5), 'Kansas City Zoo'(1). Allen worked security here while in dental school.
Location of Armacost Museum; Allen's annual scholarship fundraiser venue.
HBCU; Charles Allen, Ollie Hubbard, Georgia Buchanan attended. Key link between MNF and Buck Buchanan.
Referenced by Price, Williams, Joseph. Kansas capital; Brown v. Board connection via KC Call involvement.
Separate city from KCMO; referenced by multiple members in metro context.
Referenced by Williams, Cleaver, Joseph, Martin — general cultural/travel references.
Carl Clark and Ed West neighborhood reference.
Jerry Price's last coaching job; won KC Coaches Association head coaching award (2003) while here.
Consolidated: 'St. James'(3), 'St. James United Methodist'(2). Church of Carl Clark, Emanuel Cleaver (pastor), Buck Buchanan. Carl had his regular aisle seat.
Employer of Sypert, Price, West, Ike Gardner, Paul Booker. Ike became assistant superintendent.
Harry Sypert references Greenwood and Archer — site of 1921 Tulsa Race Massacre. Referenced in context of night MLK was assassinated.
Consolidated: 'Clarksville'(3), 'Clarksville, Arkansas'(2). Ed West's birthplace; family left when he was 3.

column4: 22nd St & Charlotte St, Kansas City, MO
column8: Near Lincoln High; neighborhood landmark. Near Municipal Stadium.
Carl Clark's grandmother's home; demolished in urban renewal.
KC's historic racial dividing line; mentioned by multiple members.
Charles Allen's third jazz club.
Cleaver's post-election mayor banquet held here.
University of Kansas location; referenced by Allen, Sypert, Price.
Consolidated: 'Prairie View'(4). Emanuel Cleaver's alma mater; where he met Otis Taylor.
Major historic boulevard in Black KC.

column4: Prospect Ave area, Kansas City, MO
column8: Don Williams walked in here asking for Carl Clark — how he found MNF. MNF gathering spot.
Carl Clark's grade school. All his childhood friends went here together.
Consolidated: 'R.T. Coles'(1), 'RT Coles'(2). Feeder school to Lincoln High; Allen transferred from here.
Elementary school referenced by multiple members.
Jim Nunnelly chaired this body after his community health and housing work.
Lloyd Greenfield's career institution (~29 years). Catastrophic explosion killed his former crew ~4 months after his retirement.
Lincoln University location. Allen, Ollie Hubbard, and Georgia Buchanan (Buck Buchanan's wife) all attended here.
Referenced by West and Joseph in KC metro context.
Consolidated: 'Norman'(3), 'OU' refs. Dr. Fisher (Sypert's Langston professor) was one of first Blacks to integrate OU law school.

column4: 1005 DB Todd Jr Blvd, Nashville, TN 37208
column8: Charles Allen applied to dental school here before attending Lincoln University.

column4: 18th St & Woodland Ave, Kansas City, MO
column8: Clark's neighborhood; Lincoln High School location.

column4: 19th St & Forest Ave, Kansas City, MO
column8: Charles Allen's birthplace; Wheatley Hospital was a historic Black hospital.
Charles Allen's childhood home.
Clark describes as KC Black social corridor.
Ed West recalls first MNF meeting on Gregory, 'across from the graveyard.'
Current MNF weekly meeting location per Jason Joseph.
Historic Black theater on 18th Street.

column4: 18th St & Woodland Ave, Kansas City, MO
column8: Carl Clark's stepfather's hat shop; Carl worked there as a child.
Carl Clark's family had property here.
Referenced by West and Greenfield.
Nunnelly's mother experienced racial discrimination in healthcare here.
Harry Sypert's former student attended here.
Harry Sypert reference to Nashville's historic Black commercial district.
Harry Sypert's activist Langston classmates came from here.
Harry Sypert's activist Langston classmates came from here.
Lloyd Greenfield's home street; pressure from nearby fire station explosion cracked his mirror.
John Martin reference; tornado damage story.
Hosts Charles Allen's annual scholarship fundraiser (~$50/person entry).

column4: 19th St & Forest Ave, Kansas City, MO
column8: Charles Allen's birthplace; historic Black hospital.
75th & Prospect — segregated amusement park recalled by Allen.
Jerry Price nearly qualified for the U.S. Open golf here.
Harry Sypert took Jets players here to socialize.
Carl Clark's family business was the first Black org to clean City Hall (~49 employees).

column4: 31st St & Benton Blvd, Kansas City, MO
column8: Nunnelly, Ike Gardner, and Charles Allen named on this memorial.
Civilian police review board; Ed West served as interim director upon returning from California.
Harry Sypert worked here while also at USDA during college.
Abner Haynes (player Price admired) attended here.

column4: 3500 John A Merritt Blvd, Nashville, TN 37209
column8: Harry Sypert reference; student he knew went here.
Ed West's son became director of recreation for the City of San Diego.
Eldridge Cleaver fled here after Cuba (per Emanuel Cleaver's account).
Eldridge Cleaver's final exile location before returning to the U.S.
Don Williams reference.
Emanuel Cleaver attended an SCLC meeting here with Daddy King (MLK Sr.).
